faculties.name: Engineering
faculties.description: Faculty of Engineering encompassing civil, mechanical, electrical, and sustainable engineering disciplines, including both research and professional programs.

faculties.name: Science and Technology
faculties.description: Faculty covering core scientific and technological disciplines such as Computer Science, Environmental Science, Physics, and related interdisciplinary areas.

faculties.name: Arts and Humanities
faculties.description: Faculty focused on historical, linguistic, literary, and philosophical scholarship, including the Department of History and related fields.

faculties.name: Social Sciences and Public Policy
faculties.description: Faculty of social sciences with departments such as Sociology, Political Science, Economics, and Public Policy, with strong emphasis on social inequality research.

faculties.name: Health Sciences
faculties.description: Faculty including Public Health and allied health disciplines, supporting research on epidemiology, health services, and global health challenges.

departments.name: Computer Science
departments.description: The Department of Computer Science conducts research and teaching in algorithms, machine learning, artificial intelligence, software systems, and data science.

departments.name: Environmental Science
departments.description: The Department of Environmental Science focuses on climate change, sustainability, ecosystems, and environmental policy research.

departments.name: Public Health
departments.description: The Department of Public Health specializes in epidemiology, global health, health systems, and population health data, including infectious disease surveillance.

departments.name: History
departments.description: The Department of History advances research on social, political, economic, and cultural histories across global regions and time periods.

departments.name: Sociology
departments.description: The Department of Sociology focuses on social inequality, institutions, culture, and quantitative and qualitative sociological methods.

departments.name: Mechanical Engineering
departments.description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering covers thermofluids, dynamics, materials, and sustainable engineering systems with strong industry collaboration.

outputs.title: Interpretable machine learning for healthcare outcomes
outputs.subtitle: Bridging transparency and performance in clinical prediction models
outputs.abstract: This article presents a framework for interpretable machine learning models for predicting healthcare outcomes using electronic health records. We compare state-of-the-art black-box models with inherently interpretable approaches and propose hybrid techniques that balance accuracy and transparency.
outputs.journalTitle: Journal of Machine Learning in Health
outputs.apaCitation: Nguyen, A., Patel, B., & Liu, G. (2021). Interpretable machine learning for healthcare outcomes: Bridging transparency and performance in clinical prediction models. Journal of Machine Learning in Health, 10(2), 45–67. https://doi.org/10.1234/unics.2021.001

outputs.title: Scalable graph neural networks for social recommendation
outputs.subtitle: Efficient neighborhood sampling and training strategies
outputs.abstract: We propose a scalable graph neural network architecture for social recommendation systems operating on large-scale user–item graphs. Our contributions include an efficient neighborhood sampling algorithm and a distributed training strategy that enables training on graphs with billions of edges.
outputs.apaCitation: Nguyen, A., Patel, B., & Mendes, C. (2022). Scalable graph neural networks for social recommendation: Efficient neighborhood sampling and training strategies. ACM Transactions on Recommender Systems, 3(1), 1–28. https://doi.org/10.1234/unics.2022.015

outputs.title: Robust machine learning under distribution shift
outputs.subtitle: Benchmarking algorithms for real-world deployment
outputs.abstract: This article benchmarks a range of algorithms for robust machine learning under covariate and label shift. Using real-world case studies from finance and healthcare, we evaluate reweighting, domain adaptation, and ensemble-based approaches, and provide guidelines for practitioners.
outputs.apaCitation: Patel, B., Nguyen, A., & Chen, D. (2023). Robust machine learning under distribution shift: Benchmarking algorithms for real-world deployment. Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research, 76(4), 210–245. https://doi.org/10.1234/unics.2023.030

outputs.title: Fairness-aware machine learning for student admissions
outputs.subtitle: Mitigating bias in predictive models of academic success
outputs.abstract: We investigate algorithmic fairness in machine learning models used for student admissions. The paper evaluates several fairness constraints and debiasing techniques on a large dataset of historical admissions and performance records.
outputs.journalTitle: International Journal of Educational Data Science
outputs.apaCitation: Chen, D., & Mendes, C. (2021). Fairness-aware machine learning for student admissions: Mitigating bias in predictive models of academic success. International Journal of Educational Data Science, 5(3), 99–123. https://doi.org/10.1234/unics.2021.040

outputs.title: Trustworthy artificial intelligence for clinical decision support
outputs.subtitle: Design principles for human-centered AI in hospitals
outputs.abstract: The paper outlines design principles and governance mechanisms for implementing trustworthy artificial intelligence in clinical decision support systems. Through interviews and case studies, we identify best practices for transparency, accountability, and user engagement.
outputs.apaCitation: Nguyen, A., Wright, O., & Liu, G. (2024). Trustworthy artificial intelligence for clinical decision support: Design principles for human-centered AI in hospitals. AI in Medicine and Health, 12(1), 1–22. https://doi.org/10.1234/unics.2024.005

outputs.title: Local climate change adaptation in coastal cities
outputs.subtitle: Governance challenges and policy pathways
outputs.abstract: Focusing on three coastal cities, this paper analyzes local climate change adaptation strategies with an emphasis on governance challenges, stakeholder engagement, and policy integration across scales.
outputs.apaCitation: Ross, E., & Khan, F. (2021). Local climate change adaptation in coastal cities: Governance challenges and policy pathways. Environmental Policy and Governance, 31(3), 233–252. https://doi.org/10.1234/unienvs.2021.010

outputs.title: Community-based climate change adaptation and resilience
outputs.subtitle: Evidence from participatory planning in rural regions
outputs.abstract: This study evaluates community-based climate change adaptation initiatives in rural regions, focusing on participatory planning, local knowledge integration, and social resilience outcomes.
outputs.apaCitation: Ross, E., Khan, F., & Wright, O. (2021). Community-based climate change adaptation and resilience: Evidence from participatory planning in rural regions. Climate and Development, 13(8), 654–672. https://doi.org/10.1234/unienvs.2021.025

outputs.title: Financing climate change adaptation through green bonds
outputs.subtitle: Risks and opportunities for emerging economies
outputs.abstract: The paper examines the role of green bonds in financing climate change adaptation projects in emerging economies, assessing financial risks, governance structures, and policy frameworks.
outputs.apaCitation: Khan, F. (2021). Financing climate change adaptation through green bonds: Risks and opportunities for emerging economies. Journal of Sustainable Finance, 9(4), 389–410. https://doi.org/10.1234/unienvs.2021.030

outputs.title: Social inequality and urban transformation in twentieth-century Europe
outputs.subtitle: Housing, labor, and welfare in three industrial cities
outputs.abstract: This doctoral thesis explores the relationship between social inequality and urban transformation in three European industrial cities during the twentieth century, focusing on housing policy, labor markets, and welfare regimes.
outputs.apaCitation: Owens, I. (2015). Social inequality and urban transformation in twentieth-century Europe: Housing, labor, and welfare in three industrial cities (Doctoral thesis). University Research Repository.

outputs.title: Social inequality in the digital age
outputs.subtitle: Platforms, precarity, and the new labor question
outputs.abstract: The thesis investigates how digital platforms reshape social inequality, focusing on gig work, algorithmic management, and labor precarity in urban contexts.
outputs.apaCitation: Martinez, J. (2016). Social inequality in the digital age: Platforms, precarity, and the new labor question (Doctoral thesis). University Research Repository.

outputs.title: Gender, race, and social inequality in colonial cities
outputs.subtitle: Comparative perspectives from North Africa and South Asia
outputs.abstract: This interdisciplinary thesis examines how gender and race structures intersected with social inequality in colonial urban settings, drawing on archival research from North African and South Asian cities.
outputs.apaCitation: Al-Hassan, L. (2017). Gender, race, and social inequality in colonial cities: Comparative perspectives from North Africa and South Asia (Doctoral thesis). University Research Repository.

outputs.title: Education, mobility, and social inequality in Latin America
outputs.subtitle: A mixed-methods analysis of intergenerational change
outputs.abstract: Using mixed methods, this thesis analyzes the relationship between education expansion, social mobility, and persistent inequality in several Latin American countries from 1970 to 2010.
outputs.apaCitation: Diallo, K. (2018). Education, mobility, and social inequality in Latin America: A mixed-methods analysis of intergenerational change (Doctoral thesis). University Research Repository.

outputs.title: Memory, violence, and social inequality in post-conflict states
outputs.subtitle: Transitional justice and everyday life
outputs.abstract: This thesis explores how transitional justice mechanisms intersect with everyday experiences of social inequality in post-conflict societies, drawing on ethnographic fieldwork and oral histories.
outputs.apaCitation: Owens, I. (2019). Memory, violence, and social inequality in post-conflict states: Transitional justice and everyday life (Doctoral thesis). University Research Repository.

outputs.title: Quantitative approaches to measuring social inequality
outputs.subtitle: Big data, survey design, and index construction
outputs.abstract: The thesis evaluates quantitative methods for measuring social inequality, comparing traditional survey-based indices with big data-derived indicators and proposing best-practice guidelines.
outputs.apaCitation: Martinez, J., & Diallo, K. (2020). Quantitative approaches to measuring social inequality: Big data, survey design, and index construction (Doctoral thesis). University Research Repository.

outputs.title: COVID-19 daily cases and hospitalizations dataset (Region A)
outputs.subtitle: Anonymized line list and aggregated time series for 2020
outputs.abstract: This dataset contains anonymized line-list records and aggregated daily counts of confirmed COVID-19 cases and hospitalizations for Region A from January to December 2020.
outputs.apaCitation: Liu, G., & Schmidt, H. (2020). COVID-19 daily cases and hospitalizations dataset (Region A): Anonymized line list and aggregated time series for 2020 [Data set]. University Data Repository. https://doi.org/10.1234/uniph.2020.050

outputs.title: COVID-19 testing and positivity rates dataset (Region A)
outputs.subtitle: Aggregated testing indicators by age and sex, 2020
outputs.abstract: The dataset provides daily and weekly aggregates of COVID-19 tests performed and positivity rates in Region A during 2020, disaggregated by age group and sex.
outputs.apaCitation: Schmidt, H., & Wright, O. (2020). COVID-19 testing and positivity rates dataset (Region A): Aggregated testing indicators by age and sex, 2020 [Data set]. University Data Repository. https://doi.org/10.1234/uniph.2020.060

outputs.title: COVID-19 mobility and non-pharmaceutical interventions dataset
outputs.subtitle: Indicators of movement and policy restrictions in 2020
outputs.abstract: This dataset combines anonymized mobility metrics from mobile devices with coded non-pharmaceutical interventions for multiple regions during the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic.
outputs.apaCitation: Liu, G. (2020). COVID-19 mobility and non-pharmaceutical interventions dataset: Indicators of movement and policy restrictions in 2020 [Data set]. University Data Repository. https://doi.org/10.1234/uniph.2020.075

outputs.title: Sustainable energy systems for smart cities
outputs.subtitle: Integrating renewables, storage, and intelligent control
outputs.abstract: This conference paper presents an integrated framework for sustainable energy systems in smart cities, combining renewable generation, energy storage, and intelligent control algorithms to reduce emissions and improve reliability.
outputs.journalTitle: Proceedings of the International Conference on Sustainable Engineering
outputs.apaCitation: Bianchi, M., & Green, N. (2019). Sustainable energy systems for smart cities: Integrating renewables, storage, and intelligent control. In Proceedings of the International Conference on Sustainable Engineering (pp. 150–159). https://doi.org/10.1234/unieng.2019.020

outputs.title: Sustainable manufacturing through additive technologies
outputs.subtitle: Life-cycle assessment of 3D-printed components
outputs.abstract: The paper evaluates the environmental impacts of additive manufacturing compared to conventional machining, using life-cycle assessment of representative 3D-printed components.
outputs.journalTitle: Proceedings of the International Conference on Sustainable Engineering
outputs.apaCitation: Green, N., & Bianchi, M. (2019). Sustainable manufacturing through additive technologies: Life-cycle assessment of 3D-printed components. In Proceedings of the International Conference on Sustainable Engineering (pp. 260–269). https://doi.org/10.1234/unieng.2019.035

grants.title: Robust and Trustworthy Machine Learning for Societal Challenges
grants.description: A multi-year grant supporting research on robust, interpretable, and trustworthy machine learning methods applied to healthcare, social recommendation, and climate adaptation analytics.

grants.title: Urban Climate Governance and Local Adaptation
grants.description: Funding for comparative research on urban climate governance, including case studies of coastal cities and local adaptation strategies.

grants.title: Innovative Climate Finance Mechanisms for Adaptation
grants.description: A focused project on the design and evaluation of green bonds and other financial instruments to support climate change adaptation in emerging economies.

grants.title: COVID-19 Surveillance and Public Health Response Data Infrastructure
grants.description: Grant to develop and maintain high-quality datasets on COVID-19 cases, hospitalizations, mobility, and non-pharmaceutical interventions to inform public health decision-making.

grants.title: Sustainable Engineering for Low-Carbon Manufacturing and Cities
grants.description: Support for research on sustainable energy systems and low-carbon manufacturing, including smart city integration and additive manufacturing life-cycle assessment.
